---
title: "ggplotting"
format: html
---

Load libraries and load in data

```{python}
import polars as pl
import numpy as np
from lets_plot import *
LetsPlot.setup_html(isolated_frame=True)


url = "https://byui-cse.github.io/cse110-course/lesson11/life-expectancy.csv"
df = pl.read_csv(url)

#If you have a Mac and have an SSL error, open spotlight (Cmd-Space), then search for "Install Certificates.command", after running the command, restart VS Code
df
```

REVIEW EXERCISE:
Just look at the United States, rename the last column to "Life".

```{python}
usa = df.filter(pl.col('Code') == 'USA').rename({'Life expectancy (years)': 'Life'})
usa
```

## Visualizations

```{python}
(
ggplot(data=usa) + geom_histogram(mapping=aes
(x='Life'))
)
```


```{python}
(
    ggplot(data = usa)
    + geom_line(mapping=aes(x='Year', y='Life'))
    #+ scale_x_continuous(limits=[1900,2000])
    + coord_cartesian(xlim=[1900,2000])

)
```


```{python}
(
    ggplot(data=usa)
    + geom_area(mapping=aes(x='Year', y='Life'))
    + scale_x_continuous(limits=[1900,2000])
    + geom_rect(xmin=1915, xmax=1920, ymin=45, ymax=56, fill='red', alpha=.2)
    + geom_segment(x=1925, xend=1920, y=46, yend=47, arrow=arrow())
    + geom_text(x=1926, y=46, label="Spanish Flu", hjust=0)
    + geom_vline(xintercept=1943, color='red', linetype=2)
    + labs(title='United States', y='Life Expectancy (Years)')
)
```
